figures.caption: Fig. 10: Examples of overtaking trajectories. The positions of the overtaking and obstacle cars are causally color coded.
Fig. 2: Simulated track layouts. (a) Training Track. (b) OOD Track 1. (c) OOD Track 2.
Fig. 9: Trajectories on hardware at different constant velocities.
Fig. 8: Zero-shot trajectories in OOD physical track configurations. A video can be viewed at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DVxlOARi4aY.
TABLE XI: Neural Activation Saturation Rates by Driving Stage
Fig. 13: Tire dynamics: Lateral acceleration across the slip angle range.

Fig. 7: Telemetry at the curriculum and unconstrained velocities.

Fig. 11: Example of an overtake motion sequence. (a) The overtaking car switches lane to traverse side-by-side. (b) Ahead before the corner apex on the outside. (c) The obstacle car is cleared and the overtaking car navigates back to the racing line. (d) The overtake is complete.
